sections.heading: Executive summary
paragraphs: Project Alder is a profitable vertical software business serving specialty logistics operators, with recurring revenue contracts, a concentrated customer base, and meaningful cross-sell potential across its payments and analytics modules. All company data in this memo is synthetic and illustrative.

The business generated FY2025 revenue of $48.2 million at an EBITDA margin of 22.4%, with revenue growth of 17.8% year over year. Net revenue retention has remained above 108% in each of the last four quarters.

The first-pass case is credible: durable revenue, healthy margins, and an identifiable cross-sell motion. It depends, however, on retention assumptions and margin progression that require diligence before the committee can rely on it. Three figures in this memo are flagged for verification.

sections.heading: Financial overview
paragraphs: The table below lists every figure used in this memo together with its source and review status. Cell-level lineage is demonstrated only for the Excel-sourced figures; filing, transcript, note, and CSV references are section- or page-level.

sections.heading: Key investment considerations
bullets: Recurring revenue: multi-year platform contracts with embedded workflow, giving high revenue visibility.
Retention: net revenue retention has remained above 108% in each of the last four quarters, indicating an expanding installed base.
Cross-sell potential: payments and analytics modules attach to the core platform and are early in their penetration curve.
Market pricing: comparable vertical-software transactions cleared at 4.9x-6.8x EV/Revenue per the vendor export; this range is flagged pending a comparability review.

sections.heading: Principal risks
bullets: Customer concentration: the top ten customers account for more than 40% of revenue, so the loss of one or two anchor accounts would materially affect the plan.
Margin progression: management guidance implies EBITDA margin expansion of roughly 150-200 bps by FY2027, but this is derived from qualitative MD&A language and needs analyst confirmation.
Retention dependence: the base case assumes retention holds at recent levels; cohort-level evidence has not yet been reviewed.
Financing: the base case assumes net leverage of 4.0x at close, which is unresolved pending lender feedback.

sections.heading: Open diligence items
bullets: Confirm the net leverage assumption (4.0x at close) with financing counterparties — currently unresolved.
Verify comparability of the comps.csv transaction set before quoting the 4.9x-6.8x range.
Obtain a management margin bridge supporting the implied 150-200 bps expansion to FY2027.
Run cohort-level retention analysis to substantiate the above-108% net revenue retention claim.

sections.heading: Review checklist reference
paragraphs: A machine-generated review checklist accompanies this memo (Project_Alder_Review_Checklist.md). Nine approved sources were used; one candidate source was excluded under the source policy; three figures are flagged for verification. This memo is a draft for review and carries no reviewer sign-off.
